column2: Adds a second Stationary Assembler so each cheap robot dedicates to one port station: Assembler 1 at Port A
column4: Dexterous Arm at Route and Clip. Counterintuitively
column5: Part Cycle Time rises vs. Case 3. The dedicated Assembler at Port A grabs the next cable the moment Station 1 clears — but Route and Clip is still occupied
column6: so the cable sits blocked at Port A for 8 ticks. That inter-station wait inflates Part CT. The extra Assembler moves the queue from "robot idle" to "part blocked" without relieving the real bottleneck: the 16-tick Arm at Route and Clip. Total throughput improves slightly (lower Total Ticks) but at a higher fleet cost and worse per-part latency — a classic local-optimization trap.
